contentLabelEntry.definition: Takes an existing object and uses it in a new context (e.g., adds an item to the cart, adds a user to a group, or adds a document to a folder).
contentLabelEntry.usage: Where appropriate, combine Add with the object (e.g., Add user, Add role). Compare Create, Insert, New, and Upload.

contentLabelEntry.definition: Stops the current action and closes the dialog.
contentLabelEntry.usage: Warn the user of any possible negative consequences of stopping an action from progressing, such as data corruption. Compare Reset.

contentLabelEntry.definition: Makes a new object from scratch (e.g., creates a calendar event or creates a new document).
contentLabelEntry.usage: In scenarios where the user needs to supply some details or settings as part of the create process, use New to initiate the action and Create to apply the user-supplied details or settings to the new object.

contentLabelEntry.definition: Destroys an existing object so that it no longer exists (e.g., deletes a file from a directory or deletes a value from a table cell).
contentLabelEntry.usage: Where appropriate, combine Delete with the object (e.g., Delete column, Delete row). Use Move to trash if the user can recover the object later.
contentLabelEntry.context: Destructive actions, danger buttons, confirmation dialogs

contentLabelEntry.definition: Allows data or values to be changed.
contentLabelEntry.usage: Use Edit to enter an editing mode. Use Update or Save for the action that applies the changes.
contentLabelEntry.context: Inline actions, page headers, card actions

contentLabelEntry.definition: Removes an object from the current context but the object is not destroyed as a result of the action (e.g., removes a user from a group or removes an item from the cart).
contentLabelEntry.usage: Where appropriate, combine Remove with the object that will be removed (e.g., Remove user, Remove role). Compare Delete which destroys the object entirely.

contentLabelEntry.definition: Saves pending modifications made to a file or document. Does not close the window or panel.
contentLabelEntry.usage: Compare Apply (saves changes without closing) and Save as (creates a new object based on the current state).
contentLabelEntry.context: Form actions, editing flows, document editing

contentLabelEntry.definition: Closes the current page or window (e.g., closing a secondary window containing online help).
contentLabelEntry.usage: Do not use Close alongside OK or Cancel actions. Compare Cancel and Done.

localizationEntry.description: For right-to-left (RTL) languages, the entire component is mirrored horizontally. Labels are right-aligned and icons that were on the left move to the right. Icons that indicate directionality (arrows, progress indicators) must also be mirrored. Icons that are not directional (checkmarks, close icons) should NOT be mirrored.

Localization can lengthen text by 20 to 30 percent for Western European languages, and more for languages like German or Finnish. CJK languages may be shorter. Avoid truncating labels whenever possible — stack buttons vertically when they cannot be displayed side by side. Test all layouts at maximum expected expansion.

Never construct labels by concatenating strings (e.g., `action + ' ' + object`). Sentence structure, word order, and grammatical agreement differ across languages. Use complete, translatable strings with placeholder tokens for dynamic content.

Do not assume two plural forms (singular and plural). Some languages have up to six plural forms (e.g., Arabic). Use ICU MessageFormat or equivalent pluralization libraries that support CLDR plural rules. Never use conditional logic like `count === 1 ? 'item' : 'items'`.

content.description: Standard action labels and localization guidelines for the Acme Design System. All UI labels should use sentence case. Prefer the {verb} + {noun} content formula on buttons except for common actions like Done, Close, Cancel.

labels.definition: Takes an existing object and uses it in a new context.
labels.usage: Combine Add with the object (e.g., Add user, Add role).

labels.definition: Stops the current action and closes the dialog.
labels.usage: Warn the user of any possible negative consequences. Compare Reset.

labels.definition: Makes a new object from scratch.
labels.usage: Use New to initiate the action and Create to apply user-supplied settings.

labels.definition: Destroys an existing object so that it no longer exists.
labels.usage: Combine Delete with the object (e.g., Delete column). Use Move to trash if recoverable.

labels.definition: Saves pending modifications. Does not close the window or panel.

localization.description: For RTL languages, the entire component layout is mirrored horizontally. Directional icons (arrows, chevrons) must be flipped. Non-directional icons (close, checkmark) must not be flipped.
Localization can lengthen text by 20-30%. Avoid truncating labels. Stack buttons vertically when they cannot be displayed side by side.
Never construct labels by concatenating strings. Use complete, translatable strings with placeholder tokens for dynamic content.
